label: Mid-1980s America
description: Reagan era prosperity. Japanese imports arrived. MTV culture. Neon and bold graphics everywhere. Self-service gas standard.

specific_models: 1982-1988 Chevrolet Celebrity — boxy front-wheel drive sedan
1986-1991 Ford Taurus — aerodynamic, rounded, revolutionary look
1981-1988 Oldsmobile Cutlass Ciera — boxy, formal roofline
1983-1987 Honda Accord — clean lines, pop-up headlights
1984-1987 Toyota Corolla — boxy compact, reliable look
1986-1989 Nissan Sentra — small economy boxy sedan
1980-1985 Cadillac Seville — sharp formal lines, bustleback
1980-1986 Ford F-150 — square body, dual headlights
1984-1988 Toyota Pickup — small, boxy, popular import

fuel.context: Prices dropped from early 1980s peak, self-service now dominant

characteristics: mirrored glass panels on newer buildings
electronic digital price signs replacing mechanical
credit card readers not yet at pump
bold primary colors with high contrast
digital price displays at gas stations
signage.typography_style: bold geometric sans-serif, early computer-influenced fonts

characteristics: green highway signs with white lettering

items: pay phones — standalone booths and wall mounted
metal bus stop shelters with advertising panels

characteristics: cable TV lines added to poles

advertising.style: high contrast, bold colors, geometric shapes, early computer graphics influence

characteristics: parking lot islands with young trees

lighting.street_lights: high pressure sodium vapor — distinctive orange glow
lighting.signs: neon and backlit plastic dominant, early LED not yet present

photography.style: slightly oversaturated, bright daylight, commercial feel

downtown_street.narrative: A 1985 main street in transition — neon and backlit plastic overtaking hand-painted signs, a first small video rental shop among the old local stores.
storefronts: small newly opened video rental shop with a 'VIDEO CASSETTE RENTALS — VHS & BETA' backlit sign
the same local diner, now with a backlit sign and 'HOT COFFEE 45¢' in the window
barber shop with its spinning barber pole, unchanged
electronics store with a red 'RADIOSHACK' style logo sign
record store with 'CASSETTES — LPs' in neon-accented window lettering
video arcade with a 'GAMES 25¢' neon sign and posters in the window
drug store modernized with backlit plastic channel letters reading 'PHARMACY'
hair salon with a neon script 'STYLES' sign
people_activities: a teenager carrying a boombox on his shoulder
a woman with big permed hair and shoulder pads window-shopping
two kids comparing rental boxes outside the video shop
a businessman in a boxy suit walking briskly with a briefcase
a man in a windbreaker reading a newspaper by the corner
a shopkeeper arranging a sidewalk sale rack
a jogger in a bright tracksuit and headband passing through
a kid locking a BMX bike to a signpost
a woman using the wall-mounted payphone
extras: a wall-mounted payphone with a steel-cord handset and phone book shelf
the painted ghost-sign wall ad now noticeably fading, colors washing out
movie posters taped inside the video shop windows
a coin-operated newspaper vending box on the corner

gas_station.narrative: A 1985 self-service gas station — digital price sign newly installed, a small convenience store where the second service bay used to be.
storefronts: flat canopy over two pump islands with angular 1980s brand striping
electronic digital price sign on the road pole, replacing mechanical numerals
self-service pumps with early digital readouts
small attached convenience store with beer and cigarette ads in the window
ice freezer chest with an 'ICE $1' decal by the store door
propane tank exchange cage beside the building
one remaining service bay with a 'STATE INSPECTION' sign
people_activities: a driver self-serving at the pump
a customer walking into the store counting bills
a clerk visible at the register through the store window
a man checking his oil with the hood up at the edge of the lot
a woman squeegeeing her windshield at the island
a teenager filling a bike tire at the air hose
a driver waiting in his car for a free pump
extras: squeegee buckets at the pump islands
a display rack of windshield-washer fluid jugs
an 'AIR & WATER' sign at the lot corner

strip_mall.narrative: A 1985 strip mall at its peak — striped awnings, neon accents, every unit leased and the lot busy.
storefronts: video rental store with a bold backlit 'VIDEO RENTALS — VHS' sign and movie posters
tanning salon with a neon palm-tree window sign
pizza parlor with a red-and-green striped awning and a neon 'PIZZA' sign
one-hour photo shop with a 'FILM DEVELOPING — 1 HOUR' sign
electronics store with a red 'RADIOSHACK' style sign
card and gift shop with gold script lettering
aerobics studio with 'AEROBICS' in neon script
nail salon with airbrushed window art
people_activities: a woman pushing a cart to a boxy sedan
teenagers clustered outside the video store
a mother with two kids heading into the gift shop
a man carrying stacked pizza boxes to his car
a woman in leg warmers entering the aerobics studio
a man loading an electronics box into a hatchback
a customer at the photo counter visible through the glass
a skateboarder rolling along the covered walkway
extras: a bank of wall-mounted payphones near the anchor store
a cart corral in the parking lot
coin-operated newspaper boxes by the walkway
striped awnings casting bold shadows on the walkway

default.narrative: An ordinary 1985 American commercial street — bold primary-color signage, boxy cars, the first franchise logos among local shops.
storefronts: local diner with a backlit sign and 'HOT COFFEE 45¢' in the window
barber shop with its spinning barber pole
small video rental shop with a 'VHS RENTALS' backlit sign
pharmacy with backlit plastic channel letters
electronics store with a red logo sign
pizza shop with a neon 'PIZZA' window sign
people_activities: a woman with permed hair and shoulder pads window-shopping
a businessman in a boxy suit with a briefcase
a teenager with a boombox on his shoulder
a shopkeeper arranging a sidewalk rack
kids on BMX bikes by the curb
a jogger in a bright tracksuit
a woman using a wall-mounted payphone
a couple leaving the diner
extras: the painted ghost-sign wall ad, fading
